title: Dependency direction should follow stability
description: Stable, abstract policy should not depend on volatile, concrete detail; dependencies pointing the wrong way propagate churn upward.
body: Use when deciding which module should depend on which. Modules that change rarely and hold core policy should be depended upon, not depend on volatile detail like specific I/O or vendors; when high-level policy depends directly on a concrete detail, every detail change ripples up. Do not let core logic import a specific database/vendor client directly. Actions: invert the dependency with an interface owned by the stable side so detail depends on policy. Validation signals include the volatile module importing the abstraction, not vice versa. Risk boundary: a stable module depending on a volatile one guarantees recurring breakage; treat wrong-direction dependencies on core policy as a design defect.
